epigraph.quote: The thatched house have irises growing out of the roofridge.

per_stop.quote: the ferry people had given our tickets to other people called Kirby, who had not taken their booking number off the internet.
Windy, high cypress & privet hedges. Typical French summer camp chalets. Pretty.
vast pots of mussels, moules. Simon ate mine. Sharp white wine.
there was a gambling casino but I couldn't find a postard.
They were clearing up the market. It rained. We left.
The garden was very pretty with birdcages in + beech hedges (+ gnomes!).
Honfleur has a beautiful harbour + a very straight, uphill road leading out of it.
We are on the edge of a steep slope down to the sea. We walk down steep steps to walk on the sandy beach near the gulls.
It was the start of the Allied offensive back into Europe on D Day.
It is one of the prettiest campsites that I have ever seen.
The Americans trying to scale the cliffs at Omaha used ladders from the London fire department.
We saw the Bayeux Tapestry, very clearly laid out, Beautiful stitching of English wool.
Wonderful round Norman arches & huge space inside the church. Honey-coloured stone.
Coutances, which has a magnificent cathedral. Vertical columns go right down across the windows on the exterior.
We stopped at Lessay which also has a fabulous abbey, very severe.
